# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent postgres weak auth.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf - Production Configuration
# Generated for application database deployment
#
# IMPORTANT: Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins.
# Ensure this file has restricted permissions (mode 0600, owned by postgres).
#
# Documentation: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/auth-pg-hba-conf.html

# =============================================================================
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD
# =============================================================================

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOCAL CONNECTIONS (Unix domain socket)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Deny all local connections for the postgres superuser by default.
# Superuser access should be tightly controlled and audited.
# Use 'peer' only from a dedicated admin jump host or session.
local   all             postgres                                reject

# Allow local peer authentication for the admin role.
# 'peer' uses OS-level username matching — no password transmitted.
# The connecting OS user must match the PostgreSQL role name exactly.
local   all             db_admin                                peer

# Application service account — local socket connections via peer auth.
# The application process must run as the OS user 'app_service'.
local   app_production  app_service                             peer

# Readonly/reporting role — local socket with peer auth.
local   app_production  readonly_user                           peer
local   reporting       readonly_user                           peer

# Deny all other local connections not explicitly permitted above.
local   all             all                                     reject

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOCAL LOOPBACK (IPv4 and IPv6)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Admin connections from localhost only — require scram-sha-256.
# MD5 is considered weak; scram-sha-256 is the modern secure alternative.
host    all             db_admin        127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             db_admin        ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# Application connections from localhost.
host    app_production  app_service     127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    app_production  app_service     ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# Reject all other loopback connections.
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            reject
host    all             all             ::1/128                 reject

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HOST-BASED AUTHENTICATION — Application Server Network
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Admin role — restricted to dedicated admin/bastion subnet.
# Only scram-sha-256 with TLS required (hostssl enforces SSL).
hostssl all             db_admin        10.0.10.0/28            scram-sha-256

# Application servers subnet — app_service role with SSL required.
# Restrict to the application database only.
hostssl app_production  app_service     10.0.20.0/24            scram-sha-256

# Read-only replicas / reporting tools subnet — SSL required.
hostssl app_production  readonly_user   10.0.30.0/24            scram-sha-256
hostssl reporting       readonly_user   10.0.30.0/24            scram-sha-256

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HOST-BASED AUTHENTICATION — Monitoring and Backup
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Monitoring agent (e.g., pg_exporter) — dedicated monitoring subnet.
hostssl monitoring      monitoring_user 10.0.40.0/28            scram-sha-256

# Backup service (e.g., pgBackRest, Barman) — dedicated backup subnet.
hostssl all             backup_user     10.0.50.0/28            scram-sha-256

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REPLICATION CONNECTIONS
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Streaming replication from known replica servers — SSL required.
hostssl replication     replication_user 10.0.60.0/28           scram-sha-256

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CERTIFICATE-BASED AUTHENTICATION (optional, highest security tier)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# If client certificates are deployed, uncomment the following lines
# to require mutual TLS (mTLS) for admin connections:
#
# hostssl all           db_admin        10.0.10.0/28            cert    clientcert=verify-full
# hostssl replication   replication_user 10.0.60.0/28           cert    clientcert=verify-full

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DEFAULT DENY — Reject everything else
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reject any host connection not matched above (IPv4).
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject

# Reject any host connection not matched above (IPv6).
host    all             all             ::/0                    reject

# Reject any SSL host connection not matched above (IPv4).
hostssl all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject

# Reject any SSL host connection not matched above (IPv6).
hostssl all             all             ::/0                    reject

# Reject non-SSL host connections globally (defense in depth).
hostnossl all           all             0.0.0.0/0               reject
hostnossl all           all             ::/0                    reject

# =============================================================================
# COMPANION postgresql.conf SETTINGS (apply in postgresql.conf, not here):
# =============================================================================
#
# # Enforce scram-sha-256 as the password hashing method:
# password_encryption = 'scram-sha-256'
#
# # Require SSL for all connections:
# ssl = on
# ssl_cert_file = '/etc/postgresql/ssl/server.crt'
# ssl_key_file = '/etc/postgresql/ssl/server.key'
# ssl_ca_file = '/etc/postgresql/ssl/ca.crt'
# ssl_min_protocol_version = 'TLSv1.3'
# ssl_ciphers = 'TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256'
#
# # Listen only on required interfaces (not 0.0.0.0 unless behind firewall):
# listen_addresses = '10.0.0.5,127.0.0.1'
#
# # Log all connection attempts for auditing:
# log_connections = on
# log_disconnections = on
# log_hostname = off
#
# # Limit connection slots per role:
# # Use ALTER ROLE ... CONNECTION LIMIT N;
#
# =============================================================================
# END OF pg_hba.conf
# =============================================================================
